req.title: System-Admin deletes team
req.statement: An authenticated system-admin shall be able to delete a team through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: Test suite validates system-admin team deletion capability across positive (successful deletion with audit), negative (auth/role failures), and boundary (empty/null team_id) scenarios. Design applies EP to partition valid/invalid inputs, NEG for auth/role violations, BVA for edge-case identifiers, and DT to map role/auth state combinations. All tests are self-contained, deterministic, and idempotent via cleanup and mocked state.

tests.name: test_system_admin_deletes_team_success
preconditions: Team with ID 'team-001' exists in system
actions: Query audit log for deletion record
expected: Audit record created with action='DELETE', actor='admin', entity_id='team-001'
Team no longer exists in database
trace: R-WEB-011: authenticated system-admin shall delete team
R-WEB-011: audit record written on success
tests.executable:
import requests
import json
from datetime import datetime

def test_system_admin_deletes_team_success():
    base_url = "http://localhost:8080"
    admin_token = <auth_fixture_admin_token>
    team_id = "team-001"
    
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_token}"}
    resp = requests.delete(f"{base_url}/teams/{team_id}", headers=headers)
    
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["entity_id"] == team_id
    
    audit_resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/audit",
        params={"entity_id": team_id, "action": "DELETE"},
        headers=headers
    )
    audit_records = audit_resp.json()
    assert len(audit_records) > 0
    assert audit_records[0]["actor"] == "admin"
    
    verify_resp = requests.get(f"{base_url}/teams/{team_id}", headers=headers)
    assert verify_resp.status_code == 404

tests.name: test_delete_team_unauthenticated_returns_401
expected: Response includes error message about missing/invalid authentication
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_delete_team_unauthenticated_returns_401():
    base_url = "http://localhost:8080"
    team_id = "team-002"
    
    resp = requests.delete(f"{base_url}/teams/{team_id}")
    
    assert resp.status_code == 401
    body = resp.json()
    assert "authentication" in body.get("error", "").lower() or \
           "unauthorized" in body.get("error", "").lower()
    
    verify_resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/teams/{team_id}",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {<auth_fixture_admin_token>}"}
    )
    assert verify_resp.status_code == 200

tests.name: test_delete_team_insufficient_role_returns_403
preconditions: Admin role is required for deletion
actions: Verify HTTP response status and error
expected: Response includes error about insufficient permissions
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_delete_team_insufficient_role_returns_403():
    base_url = "http://localhost:8080"
    user_token = <auth_fixture_user_token>
    team_id = "team-003"
    
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {user_token}"}
    resp = requests.delete(f"{base_url}/teams/{team_id}", headers=headers)
    
    assert resp.status_code == 403
    body = resp.json()
    assert "permission" in body.get("error", "").lower() or \
           "forbidden" in body.get("error", "").lower()
    
    admin_token = <auth_fixture_admin_token>
    verify_resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/teams/{team_id}",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_token}"}
    )
    assert verify_resp.status_code == 200

tests.name: test_delete_team_empty_team_id_returns_400
preconditions: team_id parameter is empty string or null
actions: Verify HTTP response status and validation error
expected: Response includes validation error for missing/invalid team_id
No audit record created for failed request
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_delete_team_empty_team_id_returns_400():
    base_url = "http://localhost:8080"
    admin_token = <auth_fixture_admin_token>
    
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_token}"}
    resp = requests.delete(f"{base_url}/teams/", headers=headers)
    
    assert resp.status_code == 400
    body = resp.json()
    assert "team_id" in body.get("error", "").lower() or \
           "required" in body.get("error", "").lower()
    
    audit_resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/audit",
        params={"action": "DELETE", "status": "failed"},
        headers=headers
    )
    audit_records = audit_resp.json()
    assert len(audit_records) == 0 or all(
        r.get("entity_id") != "" for r in audit_records
    )

tests.name: test_delete_nonexistent_team_returns_404
preconditions: Team with ID 'team-nonexistent-uuid' does not exist
expected: No audit record created (team did not exist to delete)
trace: R-WEB-011: boundary condition - deleting non-existent entity
R-WEB-011: error handling for missing resource
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_delete_nonexistent_team_returns_404():
    base_url = "http://localhost:8080"
    admin_token = <auth_fixture_admin_token>
    team_id = "team-nonexistent-uuid"
    
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_token}"}
    resp = requests.delete(f"{base_url}/teams/{team_id}", headers=headers)
    
    assert resp.status_code == 404
    body = resp.json()
    assert "not found" in body.get("error", "").lower() or \
           "does not exist" in body.get("error", "").lower()
    
    audit_resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/audit",
        params={"entity_id": team_id, "action": "DELETE"},
        headers=headers
    )
    audit_records = audit_resp.json()
    assert len(audit_records) == 0

tests.name: test_delete_team_with_special_chars_in_id
preconditions: Team exists with ID containing special characters: 'team-@#$%'
actions: Verify proper handling of special characters
expected: Special characters properly decoded and matched
trace: R-WEB-011: boundary validation for special input characters
tests.executable:
import requests
from urllib.parse import quote

def test_delete_team_with_special_chars_in_id():
    base_url = "http://localhost:8080"
    admin_token = <auth_fixture_admin_token>
    team_id = "team-@#$%"
    encoded_id = quote(team_id, safe="")
    
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_token}"}
    resp = requests.delete(
        f"{base_url}/teams/{encoded_id}",
        headers=headers
    )
    
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["entity_id"] == team_id
    
    audit_resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/audit",
        params={"entity_id": team_id, "action": "DELETE"},
        headers=headers
    )
    assert len(audit_resp.json()) > 0
